retrieval_and_qa.answer:
The document says Endeavor is a nonprofit founded by Linda Rottenberg and Peter Kellner, framed as entrepreneurship for emerging markets, especially Latin America. [CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:0|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260330T002742463246Z-df955600|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=0|page=1|start_char=0|end_char=992]

It also says Endeavor was portrayed as more than a simple nonprofit, acting as a relay point linking financiers, tech executives, political operators, and ambitious founders across the Americas, and elsewhere suggests it operated as a “multiplier system as much as an accelerator.” [CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:2|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260330T002742463246Z-df955600|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=2|page=1|start_char=1600|end_char=2597] [CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:8|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260330T002742463246Z-df955600|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=8|page=2|start_char=6400|end_char=7396]

Regarding Argentina, the document says Argentina became one of the clearest stages for this network, and Endeavor Argentina drew in Marcos Galperin of MercadoLibre. [CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:2|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260330T002742463246Z-df955600|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=2|page=1|start_char=1600|end_char=2597]

About MercadoLibre, the document says Galperin’s MercadoLibre became the most visible success story and describes it as an e-commerce and payments giant often compared to Amazon and eBay. [CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:2|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260330T002742463246Z-df955600|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=2|page=1|start_char=1600|end_char=2597]

The document further says MercadoPago extended MercadoLibre’s reach, including into digital payments and crypto-adjacent finance. [CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:2|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260330T002742463246Z-df955600|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=2|page=1|start_char=1600|end_char=2597] [CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:3|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260330T002742463246Z-df955600|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=3|page=1|start_char=2389|end_char=3385]

It also says the article tied MercadoLibre to Paxos, PYUSD, Ripio, and Mercado Bitcoin, within a wider payments-and-stablecoin corridor running through Argentina and Brazil. [CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:3|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260330T002742463246Z-df955600|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=3|page=1|start_char=2389|end_char=3385]

The document also presents Endeavor and MercadoLibre as part of the same broader networked narrative, including a path where a founder might meet mentors through Endeavor Argentina and later sit on boards linked to MercadoLibre. [CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:8|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260330T002742463246Z-df955600|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=8|page=2|start_char=6400|end_char=7396]
retrieval_and_qa.question: What does the document say about Endeavor and MercadoLibre?

[CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:0|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260330T002742463246Z-df955600|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=0|page=1|start_char=0|end_char=992]

It also says Endeavor was portrayed as more than a simple nonprofit, acting as a relay point linking financiers, tech executives, political operators, and ambitious founders across the Americas, and elsewhere suggests it operated as a “multiplier system as much as an accelerator.” [CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:2|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260330T002742463246Z-df955600|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=2|page=1|start_char=1600|end_char=2597] [CITATION|chunk_id=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf:8|run_id=unstructured_ingest-20260330T002742463246Z-df955600|source_uri=file:///Users/ash/Documents/repos/Zoomlytics/power-atlas/demo/fixtures/unstructured/chain_of_custody.pdf|chunk_index=8|page=2|start_char=6400|end_char=7396]

retrieval_and_qa.retrieval_query_contract:
WITH node AS c, score
WHERE c.run_id = $run_id
  AND ($source_uri IS NULL OR c.source_uri = $source_uri)
WITH c, score,
     [(c)<-[:SUPPORTED_BY]-(claim:ExtractedClaim) WHERE claim.run_id = $run_id |
         {claim_text: claim.claim_text,
          roles: [(claim)-[r:HAS_PARTICIPANT]->(m:EntityMention) | {role: r.role, mention_name: m.name, match_method: r.match_method}]}
     ] AS claim_details
RETURN c.text AS chunk_text,
       c.chunk_id AS chunk_id,
       c.run_id AS run_id,
       c.source_uri AS source_uri,
       c.chunk_index AS chunk_index,
       coalesce(c.page_number, c.page) AS page,
       c.start_char AS start_char,
       c.end_char AS end_char,
       score AS similarityScore,
       [cd IN claim_details | cd.claim_text] AS claims,
       [(c)<-[:MENTIONED_IN]-(mention:EntityMention) WHERE mention.run_id = $run_id | mention.name] AS mentions,
       [(c)<-[:MENTIONED_IN]-(mention:EntityMention)-[:RESOLVES_TO]->(canonical) WHERE mention.run_id = $run_id | canonical.name] AS canonical_entities,
       claim_details

retrieval_results.content:
broader PayPal mafia, became another recurring figure. Around them were institutions
such as the Council on Foreign Relations, the World Economic Forum, the Aspen Institute, Bain Capital,
Goldman Sachs, J.P . Morgan, eBay, PayPal, and In-Q-tel. The story moved easily between philanthropy,
venture capital, public policy, and digital infrastructure, creating the impression that Endeavor was less a
simple nonprofit than a relay point linking financiers, tech executives, political operators, and ambitious
founders across the Americas.
Argentina became one of the clearest stages for this network. Endeavor Argentina drew in Marcos Galperin
of MercadoLibre, Guibert Englebienne and Martin Migoya of Globant, Maria Julia Bearzi, Eduardo Elzstain,
and later figures orbiting Javier Milei’s business-friendly circles. Galperin’s MercadoLibre became the most
visible  success  story:  an  e-commerce  and  payments  giant  often  compared  to  Amazon  and  eBay,  with
MercadoPago extending its reach 
[Claim context — explicit roles via participation edges]
  • MercadoPago extended MercadoLibre's reach. [subject='MercadoPago' (match: raw_exact)]
  • Martin Migoya was of Globant. [subject='Martin Migoya' (match: raw_exact), object='Globant' (match: raw_exact)]
  • Marcos Galperin was of MercadoLibre. [subject='Marcos Galperin' (match: raw_exact), object='MercadoLibre' (match: raw_exact)]
  • Institutions around the network included the Council on Foreign Relations, the World Economic Forum, the Aspen Institute, Bain Capital, Goldman Sachs, J.P. Morgan, eBay, PayPal, and In-Q-tel.
  • Endeavor was portrayed as less a simple nonprofit than a relay point linking financiers, tech executives, political operators, and ambitious founders across the Americas. [subject='Endeavor' (match: raw_exact)]
  • Guibert Englebienne was of Globant. [subject='Guibert Englebienne' (match: raw_exact), object='Globant' (match: raw_exact)]
  • Endeavor Argentina drew in Marcos Galperin of MercadoLibre, Guibert Englebienne and Martin Migoya of Globant, Maria Julia Bearzi, Eduardo Elzstain, and later figures orbiting Javier Milei's business-friendly circles. [subject='Endeavor Argentina' (match: raw_exact)]
  • Galperin's MercadoLibre became the most visible success story. [subject='MercadoLibre' (match: raw_exact)]

Graph Demo Ingestion
Condensed unstructured sample adapted from the attached source article
Demo note: This version is intentionally simplified and lightly fictionalized for entity extraction, relationship
extraction, and graph retrieval testing. It preserves many of the people, companies, places, and institutional
links mentioned in the source while keeping a narrative, article-like style.
In  the  late  1990s,  a  network  began  to  take  shape  around  Endeavor ,  the  nonprofit  founded  by  Linda
Rottenberg and Peter Kellner . The project was framed as entrepreneurship for emerging markets, especially
Latin America, but the cast around it quickly stretched from Buenos Aires and Santiago to New York,
Washington, Silicon Valley, and Davos. Rottenberg, who had worked in Argentina and moved through circles
connected  to  Ashoka  and  Bill  Drayton,  presented  Endeavor  as  a  bridge  for  founders  who  were  too
ambitious for

pillar to the network. Founded in Buenos Aires, it grew into a software, AI, and

from this material could connect Globant not just to Argentina but to California, New York, London, and

claim_details.claim_text: Globant added a second pillar to the network.
Globant was founded in Buenos Aires.
Casares was introduced to investors in New York, Boston, and San Francisco through roadshows.
Patagon was later sold to Santander.
Globant served clients such as Google, Disney, Electronic Arts, Nestle, and American Express.
The companies were different, but the board members, mentors, sponsors, and conference circuits kept overlapping.
Globant grew into a software, AI, and digital transformation company.
The Southern Cone felt like an unusually dense cluster of founders, venture vehicles, and crypto infrastructure firms.
Casares looks like a hinge connecting Argentine entrepreneurship, Wall Street capital, Silicon Valley prestige, and the early mythology of Bitcoin custody.
The same narrative current also carried Sebastián Serrano of Ripio, Rodrigo Benzaquen of Moneero, Andy Freire, Nicolas Szekasy, and Kaszek Ventures.

Casares looks like a hinge connecting Argentine entrepreneurship, Wall Street capital, Silicon Valley prestige, and the early mythology of Bitcoin custody.

The  cast  widened  further  with  Kiva,  Better  Than  Cash  Alliance,  the  United  Nations,  the  Rockefeller
Foundation, the Gates Foundation, Co-Develop, and digital identity efforts in places such as Sierra Leone. In
the simplified telling, these names form another layer of the same map: fintech for the unbanked, digital
IDs, blockchain rails, and payment modernization in the Global South. Omidyar’s ecosystem linked eBay and
PayPal to later nonprofit and policy initiatives, while Hoffman linked venture capital, political influence, and
platform power . The article also mentioned Howard Cox, George Tenet, Mayo Shattuck III, Larry Summers,
and other figures who made the boundary between finance, intelligence, and technology feel porous,
especially  when  companies  such  as  In-Q-tel-backed  firms,  Palantir-adjacent  networks,  or  surveillance-
oriented platforms appeared in the

By the time the narrative reached more recent years, the map included satellites, stablecoins, and national
politics. Emiliano Kargieman’s Satellogic appeared with Steve Mnuchin, Joe Dunford, Howard Lutnick, Tether
Investments,  Brock  Pierce,  and  Cantor  Fitzgerald.  The  regional  conversation  touched  Argentina,  Brazil,
Uruguay, Chile, Mexico, and the broader Latin American market, but it also kept circling back to New York,
Washington,  Silicon  Valley,  Israel,  and  global  institutions.  Figures  associated  with  the  World  Jewish
Congress, AIPAC, the Aspen Global Leadership Network, and the World Economic Forum sat near founders
building payments companies, crypto exchanges, AI consultancies, and e-commerce platforms. For a graph
database demo, that mixture is useful because
